title: GovSchema Field-to-Page-Element Mapping
description: Meta-schema for mapping.json: an OPTIONAL companion artifact, sibling to a schema.json (never a member inside it), that maps a schema's field names to candidate locator signals on the live web page presenting the form (GSP-0011). Descriptive only: it says where a value for a named field goes on the page. It MUST NOT describe how to submit, navigate, or auto-complete the form — there are no members here for a submission endpoint, a next/navigation locator, or autofill/auto-submit sequencing, and additionalProperties:false throughout makes that boundary structural, not just documented. Absence of a mapping.json has no effect on the sibling schema.json's conformance to the GovSchema Document meta-schema. This is a standards artifact published by the GovSchema foundation; it does not imply endorsement by any government.

$schema.description: URI of the GovSchema mapping meta-schema this document conforms to.

mappingVersion.description: Semantic version of THIS mapping artifact. Independent of the sibling schema's `version` — a mapping is re-versioned when locators change (a site redesign) even though the schema's fields, types, and rules did not, and vice versa. See GSP-0011 'Volatility mismatch'.

schema.description: Back-reference to the sibling GovSchema document this mapping describes. Redundant with the directory path by design (defense in depth, and it lets a mapping.json be validated in isolation without re-deriving its path).

source.description: The live web page a browser-driving agent loads to resolve these locators.

verification.description: Record of how and when these locators were last confirmed to resolve on the live page. Structurally similar to but independent of the sibling schema's `verification` block: a schema's `status: verified` is a claim about legal-content fidelity; this is a claim about DOM/selector freshness. The two MUST NOT be conflated. See practices/.

method.description: Identifier of the selector-freshness verification practice used. Example: selector-probe-v1

name.description: MUST resolve to a field `name` defined in the sibling schema.json's `fields` (referential integrity, enforced by tooling — see SPEC.md). mapping.json MAY omit fields with no stable page presence; the converse (every schema field has a mapping entry) is not required.

locators.description: Ordered list of candidate locator signals, tried in order by a consuming agent until one resolves. No single signal is authoritative or guaranteed stable.

signal.description: Locator signal kind. Open vocabulary (new signals may be added without a breaking change). Recommended, in fallback order (most to least resilient to a redesign): label-text, aria-label, name-attr, id-attr, css-selector, xpath.

value.description: The literal text, attribute value, selector, or expression for this signal.
